criteria.evidence: Constraints: Days until interview: 10 ... Rounds: Recruiter screen (30m) → Hiring-manager screen (45m: background + growth/experimentation case) → Onsite (virtual): product-sense case · experimentation/analytics case · execution/roadmap case · behavioral.
criteria.reason: Timeline (10 days) and the full round structure are captured verbatim from the JD and then actively used to drive the day-by-day cadence.

criteria.evidence: Day 8: mock-interview — timed HM growth/experimentation case ... Day 9: mock-interview — timed execution/roadmap case (this is the gap-aligned case); item 4 'Build the experimentation cheatsheet ... sharpen the strength so it's automatic ... experimentation/analytics case + HM case'.
criteria.reason: Sequencing matches the JD's round shape: stories/research front-loaded for recruiter+HM+behavioral, experimentation case (his win condition) sharpened for the HM screen, gap-aligned execution/roadmap and product-sense reserved for onsite. The experimentation case that is his best shot is front-loaded, not ignored.

criteria.evidence: Priority-queue table columns Action / Skill to run / Maps to / Must-do?; e.g. '3 | Close the product-craft gaps ... | build-curriculum | execution/roadmap + product-sense cases | ✅' and '5 | Chase per-feature impact numbers ... | (user homework) | "drive measurable impact" answers | ✅'.
criteria.reason: Every action names a concrete skill and the round/gap it serves; the queue explicitly maps the partial gaps (product craft) to the right onsite cases and the strength to the HM/analytics case.

criteria.evidence: 'Done' looks like: 'Can name the gap out loud as a credible stretch: "experimentation half I own cold; roadmap/PRD/quarterly-planning half I'm growing into" ... Each of the 5–7 stories delivered in ≤90s, on a concrete decision + a real number (or an honest ⚠️ flag, never an invented one).' and 'Product-sense case no longer a cold spot ... not just optimize an existing funnel.'
criteria.reason: Concrete, honest, falsifiable readiness bar — not platitudes. It bakes in the honest-stretch framing, the anti-fabrication rule, and a specific behavioral upgrade for the weakest case type.

criteria.evidence: No coding-grind or system-design item anywhere; instead item 6 'Product-sense reps ... (the candidate's weaker case type)' and day 5 'quarterly planning track'. SQL appears only inside the strength cheatsheet context, not over-prepped.
criteria.reason: No mis-prioritization: it doesn't prescribe heavy coding or system design for this product/leadership-flavored loop, and it weights effort toward the partial gaps (product craft, product-sense) while keeping the experimentation strength sharp rather than over-grinding it.
